title: Umami Juicy Malaysian Chestnut Mushroom Stew
description: A comforting, low‑carb Malaysian stew brimming with umami‑rich chestnut mushrooms, fermented soy flavor, and fragrant herbs.

ingredients.name: fermented soy sauce blend (tamari + soy sauce + fish sauce)

instructions: Heat the coconut oil in a deep pot over medium heat.
Add the ginger & garlic paste and sauté until fragrant, about 2 minutes.
Add the sliced red chili and curry leaves; cook for another minute.
Toss in the chicken thighs and brown them on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the chestnut mushrooms and cook until they start to release moisture.
Pour in the fermented soy sauce blend, tamarind paste, and lime juice; stir well.
Add the low‑sodium chicken broth and bring to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat to a simmer, cover, and let the stew cook for 15 minutes.
Stir in the coconut milk and black pepper; simmer uncovered for an additional 5 minutes until the sauce thickens slightly.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed; serve hot, garnished with extra curry leaves if desired.
